steps.title: Identify the formula relating mass, density, and volume
steps.content: We know the density (0.215 g/cm^3) and need to find the mass. We'll calculate the volume of the cube, then multiply by density.

steps.title: Calculate the volume of the cubic basalt sample
steps.content: The cube has an edge length of 4.000 cm. For a cube with side length $s$: $$V = s^3 = (4.000)^3 = 64.000 \text{ cm}^3$$

steps.title: Multiply density by volume to find mass
steps.content: Applying the formula with our calculated volume and given density: $$\text{Mass} = 0.215 \text{ g/cm}^3 \times 64.000 \text{ cm}^3 = 13.76 \text{ grams}$$
